data.title: Test hygiene: make pytest independent of a running Postgres (suite blocks ~6min on DB connect timeouts when container is down)

data.body: Discovered during NAVI-13 (2026-06-13). Root cause: 7 test files (test_health/ask/cli/runs_list/sap_review/static_ui/trace) drive the API via TestClient, which calls check_db against the real DATABASE_URL (Postgres 127.0.0.1:5432). When the container is down (e.g. after stop.bat), each hits psycopg connection-timeout retries -> full 'uv run pytest' takes ~392s vs ~2.5s with Postgres up. Tests pass either way (136 passed/0 failed) but the wall-clock blowup makes gate checks look hung. Fix options for planning: (a) the session/conftest fixture already provides in-memory SQLite -- route check_db / the API DB dependency through it in tests so no real DB is needed; (b) mock/skip the DB-health path under test; (c) mark DB-integration tests with a marker and a fast default. Goal: 'uv run pytest' is fast and deterministic regardless of whether Postgres/Docker is running. backlog.
